title: lavandula canariensis subsp. lancerottensis
intro: Lavandula canariensis subsp. lancerottensis, also known as the Lancerotte lavender, is a subspecies of the endemic species Lavandula canariensis found in the Canary Islands, specifically on the island of Lanzarote. This plant has been utilized for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lavandula canariensis subsp. lancerottensis include its ability to soothe anxiety, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia, as well as its use in wound healing and skin care. Lavandula canariensis subsp. lancerottensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are often employed in aromatherapy and traditional medicine.
